name: drain_delay_only
title: Move sw out of jr ra delay slot (single rule)
summary: GCC scheduled a store into the jr ra delay slot, but target keeps a `nop` there. One regfix rule reverses it.
proof: func_80078B3C (commit 2353f22) and func_80078B70 (commit 1c5d90c) -- both used this rule (after byte-arith C fix). See byte_indexing_with_drain_delay for the compound recipe.

structural: the swap signature is jr/j at index N followed by sw at index N+1
no other diffs (or only minor reg_diffs that resolve themselves once the swap is corrected)

rule_skeleton.comment: move the sw at <JR>+1 out of the jr delay slot; target keeps a nop there

notes: Run `dc.sh dump-text <func>` to find the jr index. The instruction at JR_IDX+1 will be the sw that GCC scheduled in.
near_miss_attempt.py auto-detects this signature and applies the rule.
If gen_regfix reports `Instruction swaps detected: > 1`, this is not the right recipe -- you have multiple scheduling differences, likely structural reorder needed.
